title: build: accept arguments from file with --build-arg-file
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

> /kind feature

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Allows codifying build arguments into a structured file, perhaps one named ~file.Containerargs~ `argfile.conf`

The build arg file also accepts comments starting #, so automated tooling or CI/CD workflows can monitor arguments like versions to ensure they are up-to-date.

#### How to verify it

This was the example I was testing with:

```
# Containerfile
ARG ALPINE_VERSION
FROM alpine:${ALPINE_VERSION}
```

```
# argfile.conf
# container-version: alpine
ALPINE_VERSION=3.17
```

`buildah build --build-arg-file argfile.conf -f Containerfile`

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
--build-arg-file your-args.Containerargs allows specifying --build-args from a file instead of inline in the build command
```

comments.body:
I'm assuming that in your example in the description that these lines:
```
# container-version: alpine
ALPINE_VERSION=3.17
```

are contained in the file `arguments.Containerargs`?

Thanks! I made those changes. I like the idea of `--build-arg-file` defaulting to `argfile.conf` when specified but I'm not sure how to implement it. Go is not my strong suit and I couldn't find a comparable argument to plagiarise 😅
This is a new feature @nalind @rhatdan @containers/buildah-maintainers WDYT ?

Conceptually I'm fine. with it.
I don't see much need to suggest a default name for a build args file, since the next step after that seems to be reading files with that name by default, and that would create an incompatibility with `docker build` that could catch people by surprise, so it's not a road I particularly want to go down.
